text:
Once upon a time long, long ago, there lived a girl named Marisol. She lived during the Spanish-American War. One rainy afternoon, Marisol was out picking strawberries with her favorite little sister.

As they walked home, an old man leaned against a wooden pole near the path. He looked tired.

"You remember the Spanish guitar?" Marisol asked, cheerful.

He smiled sadly. "I remember that tune."

"You don't sing anymore?" Marisol teased.

He shook his head sadly again.

"Oh no. I suppose no one remembers the beauty of those songs?"

Marisol shook her head.

"They're songs that talk about love. Lost love."

"What a beautiful song!"

Her sister clapped her hands. "Let’s sing it!"

They all sang. Songs of love. Songs of longing. Songs about everything besides love.

As they reached their house, the rain poured harder, washing away petals from the ground. Marisol noticed a tiny, dewy leaf.

"What about you?"
